sections.paragraph:
δοῦπος (δοῦπος, ὁ,) n (dou_p os_ou masc)

any dead, heavy sound, a thud, Il.; of the distant din of battle, the sound of footsteps, of the measured tread of infantry, the hum of a multitude, the roar of the sea, Hom.: rare in Trag.


Etym. The form γδουπέω, connects the word with κτύπος.
